title: Install a Replacement Gripper on the A-axis
summary: Install the correct left or right replacement gripper onto the A-axis gearbox face, secure it with the specified fasteners and torque, reconnect the cable carrier, electrical connectors, and air line, reinstall the valve cover, and leave the assembly ready for separate leveling and commissioning.
when_to_use: Use when replacing a gripper on the A-axis and the source installation section is being followed to mechanically install the replacement gripper, reconnect associated cable and air connections, and prepare for later commissioning.
not_for: Do not use if the replacement gripper has not been confirmed as the correct left or right gripper.
Do not use as a complete commissioning procedure; the source states leveling and commissioning are handled by a separate Tipper Commissioning procedure.
Do not use to restart the operator station without following the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

responsibilities: Perform lockout/tagout using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Install and torque the gripper and counter-balance hardware.
Reconnect cable carrier, electrical connectors, and air line.
Reinstall the valve cover without pinching wires.
Hand off to the referenced commissioning and operator station restart procedures.

safety_notes: Use the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before performing installation work.
The A-axis may drift down after the gripper weight is added; the source states this is acceptable.
The counter-balance weight is approximately 43 lb (19.4 kg) total and may be one or two pieces.
Do not pinch wires when reinstalling the valve cover.
Do not proceed if all 10 M6x20 screws cannot be inserted due to orientation or alignment issues.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the gripper and A-axis assembly
Torque tools capable of 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb) and 63 Nm (47 ft-lb)
Access to the referenced Tipper Commissioning and Starting The Operator Station procedures

access_required: Physical access to the A-axis gripper, gearbox face, counter-balance carriage, cable carrier, valve cover, and associated wiring/air connections
Access to the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure
Access to the referenced Tipper Commissioning procedure
Access to the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure
system_preconditions: Correct replacement gripper is identified as left or right before installation
Tipper/gripper is locked out using the referenced LOTO procedure before installation work begins

steps.title: Verify replacement gripper and position axis for alignment
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct replacement part is being installed and simplify mechanical alignment before mounting.
steps.instruction: Verify the correct replacement gripper has been selected, specifically left gripper or right gripper, and manually position the Z-axis or A-axis to simplify alignment.
steps.expected_result: The correct replacement gripper is confirmed and the axis position supports installation alignment.
steps.healthy_condition: Left/right gripper selection is confirmed and the assembly is positioned to allow easier alignment to the gearbox face.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper cannot be confirmed as the correct left or right replacement, or the axis position does not allow practical alignment.

actions.label: Confirm replacement gripper
actions.exact_action: Verify the correct replacement gripper has been selected, specifically left gripper or right gripper.

actions.label: Position axis for alignment
actions.exact_action: Manually position the Z-axis or A-axis to simplify alignment.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the selected gripper is not confirmed as the correct left or right gripper.

source_evidence.text: Verify the correct replacement gripper has been selected, specifically left gripper or right gripper, and manually position the Z-axis or A-axis to simplify alignment.
Do not proceed if the selected gripper is not confirmed as the correct left or right gripper.

steps.title: Lock out the gripper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before installation work.
steps.instruction: LOTO the gripper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
steps.expected_result: The gripper/tipper is locked out per the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied before any installation work proceeds.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO is not completed or cannot be verified.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the gripper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the gripper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Use LOTO as referenced by the source before performing installation work.

steps.title: Apply thread locker to installation fasteners
steps.purpose: Prepare the fasteners for installation as specified by the source.
steps.instruction: Apply blue Loctite® thread locker to all fasteners used in these installation steps.
steps.expected_result: All specified installation fasteners are prepared with blue Loctite® before use.
steps.healthy_condition: Fasteners to be installed in subsequent steps have thread locker applied.
steps.failure_condition: Required fasteners are installed without the specified thread locker.

actions.label: Apply blue Loctite
actions.exact_action: Apply thread locker (blue Loctite®) to all fasteners used in these installation steps.

source_evidence.text: Apply thread locker (blue Loctite®) to all fasteners used in these installation steps.

steps.title: Align gripper to A-axis gearbox face
steps.purpose: Orient the gripper correctly so the full bolt pattern can be installed.
steps.instruction: Align the gripper bolt pattern to the A-axis gearbox face and ensure the gripper and gearbox orientation allows all 10 M6x20 socket-head screws to be inserted.
steps.expected_result: The gripper is aligned to the gearbox face with the bolt pattern oriented for full fastener insertion.
steps.healthy_condition: All 10 M6x20 screw positions are accessible and alignment is maintained.
steps.failure_condition: Orientation or alignment prevents insertion of all 10 M6x20 screws.

actions.label: Align gripper and bolt pattern
actions.exact_action: Align the gripper bolt pattern to the A-axis gearbox face and ensure the gripper and gearbox orientation allows all 10 M6x20 socket-head screws to be inserted.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis gearbox face and surrounding mounting context to help orient the gripper during alignment.
Additional A-axis gearbox visual context that may help confirm mounting orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if all 10 M6x20 screws cannot be inserted due to orientation or alignment issues.

source_evidence.text: Align the gripper bolt pattern to the A-axis gearbox face and ensure the gripper and gearbox orientation allows all 10 M6x20 socket-head screws to be inserted. [Image support needed: figure or page image showing gripper-to-gearbox alignment and bolt pattern orientation.]
Stop if all 10 M6x20 screws cannot be inserted due to orientation or alignment issues.

steps.title: Install the gripper mounting fasteners
steps.purpose: Secure the gripper to the gearbox face before final torque.
steps.instruction: Install the 10 M6x20 gripper fasteners. It may be easier to install two fasteners on opposite sides of the bolt circle first, then insert the remaining fasteners.
steps.expected_result: All 10 M6x20 gripper fasteners are installed.
steps.healthy_condition: Two opposite fasteners can hold alignment while the remaining fasteners are inserted and seated.
steps.failure_condition: One or more fasteners cannot be inserted or the gripper cannot be held in alignment.

actions.label: Install 10 M6x20 screws
actions.exact_action: Install the 10 M6x20 gripper fasteners; it may be easier to install two fasteners on opposite sides of the bolt circle first, then insert the remaining fasteners.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if all 10 M6x20 screws cannot be inserted.

source_evidence.text: Install the 10 M6x20 gripper fasteners; the source notes it may be easier to install two fasteners on opposite sides of the bolt circle first, then insert the remaining fasteners.

steps.title: Torque gripper fasteners
steps.purpose: Secure the gripper to the specified torque.
steps.instruction: Tighten the 10 M6 gripper fasteners to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).
steps.expected_result: All 10 M6 gripper fasteners are tightened to the specified torque.
steps.healthy_condition: Each gripper fastener is torqued to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).
steps.failure_condition: Specified torque cannot be achieved or fastener condition prevents proper tightening.

actions.label: Torque M6 gripper fasteners
actions.exact_action: Tighten the 10 M6 gripper fasteners to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the specified torque cannot be applied to the gripper fasteners.

source_evidence.text: Tighten the 10 M6 gripper fasteners to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).

steps.title: Account for possible A-axis drift
steps.purpose: Acknowledge the source-noted behavior after gripper weight is added.
steps.instruction: Note that the A-axis may drift down after the gripper weight is added; the source states this is acceptable.
steps.expected_result: Technician recognizes that downward drift may occur after adding gripper weight.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed A-axis drift down after adding gripper weight is understood as acceptable per the source.
steps.failure_condition: Unexpected behavior beyond the source-noted acceptable drift is observed.

actions.label: Observe A-axis behavior
actions.exact_action: Note that the A-axis may drift down after the gripper weight is added; the source states this is acceptable.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if A-axis behavior appears abnormal beyond the source-noted acceptable drift.

source_evidence.text: Note that the A-axis may drift down after the gripper weight is added; the source states this is acceptable.

steps.title: Attach counter-balance weight
steps.purpose: Restore the counter-balance assembly after gripper installation.
steps.instruction: Attach the counter-balance weight to the counter-balance carriage using four M10 socket head screws with blue Loctite® and tighten to 63 Nm (47 ft-lb). The counter-balance weight is approximately 3-in. x 4-in. x 13-in., one or two pieces, and weighs a total of 43 lbs (19.4 kg).
steps.expected_result: The counter-balance weight is installed on the carriage and tightened to the specified torque.
steps.healthy_condition: Counter-balance weight is secured with four M10 socket head screws and torqued to 63 Nm (47 ft-lb).
steps.failure_condition: Counter-balance weight cannot be mounted securely or specified torque cannot be achieved.

actions.label: Install counter-balance weight
actions.exact_action: Attach the counter-balance weight to the counter-balance carriage using four M10 socket head screws with blue Loctite® and tighten to 63 Nm (47 ft-lb).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counter-balance weight location, carriage attachment area, and associated hardware context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the counter-balance weight cannot be secured with the specified hardware.
Stop if the specified 63 Nm (47 ft-lb) torque cannot be achieved.

source_evidence.text: Attach the counter-balance weight to the counter-balance carriage using four M10 socket head screws with blue Loctite® and tighten to 63 Nm (47 ft-lb). The source states the counter-balance weight is approximately 3-in. x 4-in. x 13-in., one or two pieces, and weighs a total of 43 lbs (19.4 kg). [Image support needed: figure or page image showing counter-balance location and attachment hardware.]

steps.title: Attach A-axis cable carrier to gripper
steps.purpose: Reconnect the cable carrier to the installed gripper.
steps.instruction: Attach the A-axis cable carrier to the gripper using two M3x8 flat head screws (included).
steps.expected_result: The A-axis cable carrier is attached to the gripper.
steps.healthy_condition: Cable carrier is secured to the gripper with the two included M3x8 flat head screws.
steps.failure_condition: Cable carrier cannot be attached securely to the gripper.

actions.label: Attach cable carrier
actions.exact_action: Attach the A-axis cable carrier to the gripper using two M3x8 flat head screws (included).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable carrier and cable handling points relevant to reconnecting the A-axis cable carrier to the gripper.
Cable routing context around the tip/A-axis assembly that may help identify the cable carrier path.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the cable carrier cannot be attached securely.

source_evidence.text: Attach the A-axis cable carrier to the gripper using two M3x8 flat head screws (included). [Image support needed: figure or page image showing cable carrier attachment point on the gripper.]

steps.title: Route M8 connectors through grommet
steps.purpose: Route the cable correctly into the gripper connection area.
steps.instruction: Feed the M8 connectors from the cable through the grommet one at a time, then push the end of the jacketed cable through the grommet.
steps.expected_result: The M8 connectors and jacketed cable are routed through the grommet.
steps.healthy_condition: All M8 connectors pass through the grommet one at a time and the jacketed cable end is pushed through successfully.
steps.failure_condition: Connectors or cable cannot be routed through the grommet.

actions.label: Route M8 connectors
actions.exact_action: Feed the M8 connectors from the cable through the grommet one at a time, then push the end of the jacketed cable through the grommet.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Grommet location and connector routing path for passing M8 connectors one at a time.
Cable routing path around the assembly that supports grommet routing.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the M8 connectors or jacketed cable cannot be routed through the grommet.

source_evidence.text: Feed the M8 connectors from the cable through the grommet one at a time, then push the end of the jacketed cable through the grommet. [Image support needed: figure or page image showing grommet and cable routing path.]

steps.purpose: Route the air line into the gripper connection area before final connection.

steps.expected_result: The capped air line is routed through the grommet.
steps.healthy_condition: Air line passes through the grommet without damage or obstruction.
steps.failure_condition: Air line cannot be routed through the grommet.

steps.instruction: Remove the valve cover by removing the four M4x50 socket-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The valve cover is removed and the connection area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: Valve cover is removed cleanly with all four M4x50 screws accounted for.
steps.failure_condition: Valve cover cannot be removed or hardware cannot be removed safely.

actions.label: Remove valve cover screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the valve cover by removing the four M4x50 socket-head screws.

steps.title: Remove air line cap and valve fitting plug
steps.purpose: Prepare the pneumatic connection for final air line installation.
steps.instruction: Remove the air line cap and valve fitting plug, if present.
steps.expected_result: The air line and valve fitting are ready for connection.
steps.healthy_condition: Any cap or plug present has been removed from the air line and valve fitting.
steps.failure_condition: Cap or plug cannot be removed or the fitting cannot be prepared for connection.

actions.label: Remove cap and plug
actions.exact_action: Remove the air line cap and valve fitting plug, if present.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the air line cap or valve fitting plug cannot be removed.

source_evidence.text: Remove the air line cap and valve fitting plug, if present.

steps.title: Connect supply air line
steps.purpose: Restore the pneumatic supply connection to the valve.
steps.instruction: Connect the supply air line by fully inserting the line into the valve fitting.
steps.expected_result: The supply air line is connected to the valve fitting.
steps.healthy_condition: The supply air line is fully inserted into the valve fitting.
steps.failure_condition: The air line cannot be fully inserted or does not seat in the valve fitting.

actions.label: Insert supply air line
actions.exact_action: Connect the supply air line by fully inserting the line into the valve fitting.

steps.title: Reinstall valve cover without pinching wires
steps.purpose: Close the connection area while preserving correct wire routing.
steps.instruction: Install the valve cover using the four M4x50 socket head screws, taking care not to pinch any wires along the cover edges and inserting the grommet into the notch for the tote sensor wire.
steps.expected_result: The valve cover is reinstalled with wires protected and the grommet seated in the tote sensor wire notch.
steps.healthy_condition: Valve cover is secured, no wires are pinched, and the grommet is inserted into the notch for the tote sensor wire.
steps.failure_condition: Wires are pinched, the grommet cannot be seated in the notch, or the cover cannot be installed correctly.

actions.label: Install valve cover
actions.exact_action: Install the valve cover using the four M4x50 socket head screws, taking care not to pinch any wires along the cover edges and inserting the grommet into the notch for the tote sensor wire.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Valve cover area and cable routing context relevant to avoiding pinched wires.
Connector and grommet routing context that may help confirm proper wire placement before closing the cover.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if wiring cannot be routed without pinching at the valve cover edges.
Stop if the grommet cannot be seated in the tote sensor wire notch.

source_evidence.text: Install the valve cover using the four M4x50 socket head screws, taking care not to pinch any wires along the cover edges and inserting the grommet into the notch for the tote sensor wire. [Image support needed: figure or page image showing valve cover, wire routing, and grommet notch placement.]
Stop if wiring cannot be routed without pinching at the valve cover edges or if the grommet cannot be seated in the tote sensor wire notch.

steps.title: Hand off to Tipper Commissioning procedure
steps.purpose: Direct the technician to the separate source-referenced procedure for leveling and commissioning.
steps.instruction: Refer to the Tipper Commissioning procedure for leveling and commissioning the new gripper.
steps.expected_result: The installation is handed off to the separate commissioning procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: Technician recognizes that leveling and commissioning are not completed by this installation procedure alone.
steps.failure_condition: Installation is treated as complete without performing the separate commissioning procedure.

actions.label: Reference commissioning procedure
actions.exact_action: Refer to the Tipper Commissioning procedure for leveling and commissioning the new gripper.

stop_or_escalate_if: Do not treat this procedure as complete commissioning.

source_evidence.text: Refer to the Tipper Commissioning procedure for leveling and commissioning the new gripper.
This procedure references a separate Tipper Commissioning procedure for leveling and commissioning; installation alone does not complete that work.

steps.title: Restart operator station using referenced procedure
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service using the source-referenced startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.
steps.expected_result: The operator station restart is performed using the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: Operator station restart is handled through the referenced startup procedure rather than improvised steps.
steps.failure_condition: Operator station restart cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the operator station cannot be restarted using the referenced procedure.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

success_criteria: The correct left or right replacement gripper is installed on the A-axis gearbox face.
All 10 M6x20 gripper fasteners are installed and tightened to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).
The counter-balance weight is attached with four M10 socket head screws and tightened to 63 Nm (47 ft-lb).
The A-axis cable carrier is attached to the gripper.
M8 connectors are routed through the grommet and connected to the valve, tote detect sensor, and gripper sensors as specified.
The supply air line is connected by fully inserting it into the valve fitting.
The valve cover is reinstalled with no pinched wires and the grommet seated in the tote sensor wire notch.
The assembly is ready for the separate Tipper Commissioning procedure.
healthy_conditions: Correct replacement gripper is confirmed before installation.
LOTO is applied before installation work.
All 10 M6x20 screws can be inserted with proper gripper-to-gearbox orientation.
Specified torque values are achieved on gripper and counter-balance fasteners.
A-axis drift after adding gripper weight is limited to the source-noted acceptable downward drift.
Cable and air line routing through the grommet is completed successfully.
No wires are pinched when the valve cover is reinstalled.
failure_conditions: Replacement gripper is not confirmed as the correct left or right unit.
LOTO is not performed before installation work.
All 10 M6x20 screws cannot be inserted due to orientation or alignment issues.
Specified torque cannot be achieved on required fasteners.
Cable or air line cannot be routed through the grommet.
Required connectors cannot be identified or connected.
Wiring is pinched at the valve cover edges or the grommet cannot be seated in the tote sensor wire notch.
Installation is treated as complete without separate leveling and commissioning.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the correct left or right replacement gripper cannot be confirmed.
Escalate if lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
Escalate if gripper orientation or alignment prevents insertion of all 10 M6x20 screws.
Escalate if required torque values cannot be achieved.
Escalate if connectors, cable, or air line cannot be routed or connected as specified.
Escalate if the valve cover cannot be installed without pinching wires or seating the grommet correctly.
Escalate if operator station restart cannot be completed using the referenced startup procedure.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A-axis gearbox face and mounting context.
Counter-balance weight location and attachment context.
Grommet routing, connector handling, and valve connection context.
Tip/A-axis cable routing and valve cover access context.
